hypothesis: A generative model trained to jointly predict both crystal structure and its corresponding space group classification directly from XRD patterns, using symmetry-equivariant neural network architectures that embed Wyckoff position constraints as hard inductive biases rather than post-hoc projections, will achieve higher structure recovery rates than sequential approaches (classify symmetry then optimize) because the coupled learning of symmetry and geometry will smooth the effective optimization landscape by eliminating the non-convex local minima along symmetry axes identified in this work, a prediction testable by comparing match rates against the symmetry-constrained gradient descent baseline across the same MP20 structures and noise levels used here.
